items.text: Which joints are bothering you, and have you had this same pain in these joints for months or years?
choices: New joint pain, not present before
Chronic joint pain, similar to baseline
Chronic joint pain, but this feels worse or different

items.text: Is your joint stiffness worst first thing in the morning, and if so, how long does it last?
choices: Morning stiffness more than an hour

items.text: Does the pain get worse with activity and better with rest?
choices: Worse with activity, better with rest

items.text: Do you have a fever along with the joint pain?
Has one joint become suddenly hot, red, and swollen?
choices: No acute hot, red, swollen joint
Single joint acutely hot, red, and swollen

items.text: Have you had a recent joint injection, surgery, or skin infection near this joint?
Has this pain made it harder for you to walk, get up from a chair, or care for yourself at home?
choices: New difficulty with walking or transfers

items.text: Have you fallen or injured the joint recently?
choices: Recent fall or trauma to the joint

items.text: Have you been taking NSAIDs or other pain medications regularly for this?
choices: No regular NSAID or analgesic use

items.text: Have multiple small joints in your hands or feet become swollen and painful, on both sides of your body?
Has your big toe or another single joint suddenly become extremely painful, often overnight?
choices: Sudden severe pain, often in the big toe

items.text: Inspect the affected joint for erythema, warmth, and swelling.
Palpate for a joint effusion.
Assess active and passive range of motion of the affected joint.
choices: Limited range of motion from pain or stiffness
Unable to move the joint at all

items.text: Assess for crepitus with joint movement.
Assess joint stability with stress testing when appropriate.
Assess temperature for fever.
Assess for bony deformity, joint malalignment, and muscle wasting around the joint.
Assess distal pulses, capillary refill, and sensation distal to the affected joint.
Assess gait and ability to bear weight when safe.
Examine other joints for symmetric involvement.
